title: Robust and Efficient Average Consensus with Non-Coherent Over-the-Air Aggregation
problem: Achieving average consensus in large-scale wireless multi-agent systems
traditional_approaches: Distributed linear iteration where agents linearly combine their own state values with neighbors' values
Classical studies assuming either perfect communication or imperfect communication with link-level noise, packet losses, or delay
challenges: Access control and interference management are critical for achieving efficient and accurate average consensus
Assigning orthogonal resource blocks to different nodes/links is highly inefficient for extremely large networks
proposed_solution: Over-the-air (OTA) computation that leverages signal superposition to aggregate data from distributed nodes
ota_benefits: Allows N senders to transmit simultaneously using the same frequency resources
Increases spectral efficiency by a factor of N
limitations_of_typical_ota: Relies on coherent transmission requiring perfect channel state information (CSI)
Nearly impossible to achieve constructive phase-coherent combination in fully decentralized systems with multiple receivers
Estimating CSI of all links leads to substantial signaling overhead

non_coherent_ota.mechanism: Uses energy-based coding and decoding without needing phase information
non_coherent_ota.previous_work_10: Used for wireless multi-agent optimization with decentralized gradient descent (DGD)
non_coherent_ota.previous_work_11: Used for average consensus but resulted in stochastic bias in the consensus value

proposed_approach.algorithm: Average consensus algorithm based on decentralized projected gradient descent (D-PGD) with non-coherent OTA aggregation
achievements: Achieves mean square consensus on the average of agents' initial state values without bias
Includes transmit power control and receive scaling method to accelerate convergence

evaluation: Simulation results show fast and robust convergence to average consensus
